title: B2 Upper-Intermediate English
description: Sophisticated vocabulary for upper-intermediate learners, covering abstract concepts, academic language, and nuanced expression.

cards.meaning: existing as an idea, not as a physical thing
cards.example: Justice is an abstract concept that is hard to define.

cards.meaning: to accept or admit that something is true
cards.example: She acknowledged that the report contained some errors.

cards.meaning: to gain or obtain something over time
cards.example: He acquired fluency in Japanese after years of study.

cards.meaning: to publicly support a cause or policy
cards.example: She advocates for better mental health services.

cards.meaning: to distribute resources for a specific purpose
cards.example: The government allocated funds to education.

cards.meaning: open to more than one interpretation; unclear
cards.example: His answer was ambiguous and left us confused.

cards.meaning: to increase the strength or effect of something
cards.example: Social media can amplify both good and bad messages.

cards.meaning: based on random choice rather than reason
cards.example: The selection of candidates seemed arbitrary and unfair.

cards.meaning: to evaluate or judge the quality of something
cards.example: Teachers assess students through tests and assignments.

cards.meaning: to consider something as caused by a particular source
cards.example: She attributed her success to hard work and persistence.

cards.meaning: to make something greater by adding to it
cards.example: He augmented his income by freelancing at weekends.

cards.meaning: to stop happening or doing something
cards.example: Production ceased when the factory closed down.

cards.meaning: to make something clearer and easier to understand
cards.example: Could you clarify what you mean by that statement?

cards.meaning: logical, consistent, and clearly expressed
cards.example: Her argument was coherent and well-supported by evidence.

cards.meaning: to happen at the same time or to be the same
cards.example: The festival coincides with the national holiday.

cards.meaning: to make up for a loss or give payment for harm
cards.example: The company compensated workers for their overtime.

cards.meaning: to collect and put together information from many sources
cards.example: She compiled a list of all the research articles.

cards.meaning: to obey a rule or request
cards.example: All staff must comply with health and safety regulations.

cards.meaning: to form an idea or imagine something
cards.example: He conceived the plan while travelling overseas.

cards.meaning: making something likely or possible
cards.example: A quiet environment is conducive to focused study.

cards.meaning: to arrange or set up in a particular way
cards.example: The IT team configured the new software for our system.

cards.meaning: to face a problem or challenge directly
cards.example: We must confront the issue rather than ignore it.

cards.meaning: to say or show the opposite of something
cards.example: His actions contradicted his words completely.

cards.meaning: public disagreement about something important
cards.example: The new law caused great controversy.

cards.meaning: to communicate or express an idea or feeling
cards.example: She conveyed her disappointment through her tone.

cards.meaning: to have a mutual relationship with something
cards.example: Exercise correlates with better mental health outcomes.

cards.meaning: to reach a conclusion through logical reasoning
cards.example: From the clues, the detective deduced the identity of the suspect.

cards.meaning: to show or represent in a picture or description
cards.example: The painting depicts a busy marketplace in the 17th century.

cards.meaning: to get something from a source
cards.example: This word is derived from Latin.

cards.meaning: to officially choose for a particular role or purpose
cards.example: She was designated as the team leader.

cards.meaning: to discover or identify something
cards.example: The sensors detected a rise in temperature.

cards.meaning: to depart from an expected course or standard
cards.example: Do not deviate from the approved procedure.

cards.meaning: to perceive or recognize something clearly
cards.example: It was hard to discern the truth from the rumours.

cards.meaning: to move something from its usual place
cards.example: Automation has displaced many factory workers.

cards.meaning: to change the shape or meaning of something unfairly
cards.example: The media distorted the facts of the story.

cards.meaning: detailed and complicated; to explain in more detail
cards.example: Could you elaborate on your earlier point?

cards.meaning: to draw out a response or information from someone
cards.example: The teacher elicited answers from the students.

cards.meaning: to represent or be an expression of an idea
cards.example: She embodies the values of honesty and dedication.

cards.meaning: to come out or become known
cards.example: New evidence emerged during the investigation.

cards.meaning: to make sure rules or laws are obeyed
cards.example: The police enforce traffic laws.

cards.meaning: to involve or require something as a necessary part
cards.example: The job entails a lot of travel.

cards.meaning: equal in value, amount, or meaning
cards.example: A kilometre is roughly equivalent to 0.6 miles.

cards.meaning: to develop gradually over time
cards.example: Language evolves as society changes.

cards.meaning: to deliberately leave out or not include
cards.example: Children under 12 are excluded from the event.

cards.meaning: to show or display something publicly
cards.example: The museum exhibited rare artefacts from ancient Egypt.

cards.meaning: to change irregularly and often
cards.example: Oil prices fluctuate depending on global demand.

cards.meaning: to create or develop a plan or idea systematically
cards.example: The scientists formulated a new hypothesis.

cards.meaning: to produce or create something
cards.example: Solar panels generate clean energy.

cards.meaning: a proposed explanation made on limited evidence
cards.example: The researcher tested her hypothesis through experiments.

cards.meaning: to explain using examples, pictures, or diagrams
cards.example: He used a chart to illustrate the data.

cards.meaning: to suggest something without saying it directly
cards.example: Her silence implied that she disagreed.

cards.meaning: existing as a natural or permanent feature
cards.example: There are inherent risks in any business venture.

cards.meaning: to begin or start something
cards.example: The company initiated a new training programme.

cards.meaning: to introduce new ideas or methods
cards.example: Companies must innovate to stay competitive.

cards.meaning: to establish or set up a system or practice
cards.example: The government instituted new safety regulations.

cards.meaning: to explain or understand the meaning of something
cards.example: She interpreted the data differently from her colleagues.

cards.meaning: to include or require as a necessary part
cards.example: The project involves a great deal of research.

cards.meaning: to give good reasons for a decision or action
cards.example: Can you justify the extra cost of this approach?

cards.meaning: to keep in good condition or continue at the same level
cards.example: It is important to maintain a healthy diet.

cards.meaning: to control or influence cleverly or unfairly
cards.example: He was accused of manipulating the data.

cards.meaning: to reduce something to the smallest possible amount
cards.example: We should minimize the risk of errors.

cards.meaning: to make small changes to improve something
cards.example: They modified the design based on user feedback.

cards.meaning: to watch and check something over time
cards.example: Doctors monitor patients' blood pressure regularly.

cards.meaning: to give someone a reason or desire to do something
cards.example: Good leaders motivate their teams.

cards.meaning: not supporting either side in a conflict or argument
cards.example: Switzerland remained neutral during the war.

cards.meaning: to get or gain something
cards.example: You must obtain permission before entering the building.

cards.meaning: a typical example or model of something
cards.example: The internet represented a paradigm shift in communication.

cards.meaning: to continue firmly despite difficulty
cards.example: Despite failures, she persisted and eventually succeeded.

cards.meaning: to suggest a theory as a basis for reasoning
cards.example: Darwin postulated that species evolve through natural selection.

cards.meaning: to come before something in time or order
cards.example: A warm-up should always precede intense exercise.

cards.meaning: to say what will happen in the future
cards.example: Scientists predicted the storm would hit on Thursday.

cards.meaning: widespread or common in a particular area or time
cards.example: Obesity is increasingly prevalent in developed countries.

cards.meaning: most important or main
cards.example: The principal reason for the delay was bad weather.

cards.meaning: to support or encourage something actively
cards.example: The campaign promotes healthy eating habits.

cards.meaning: to meet required standards or add conditions to a statement
cards.example: She qualified as a doctor after six years of study.

cards.meaning: to improve something by making small changes
cards.example: He refined his writing by editing it several times.

cards.meaning: to control something using rules
cards.example: The government regulates food safety standards.

cards.meaning: to strengthen or support an idea or structure
cards.example: Positive feedback reinforces good behaviour.

cards.meaning: to put into a lower position or category
cards.example: The team was relegated to a lower division.

cards.meaning: to reconsider or change something after checking it
cards.example: Please revise your essay based on my comments.

cards.meaning: happening or done at the same time
cards.example: The film was released simultaneously in 50 countries.

cards.meaning: to state something clearly and exactly
cards.example: Please specify the date and time of the meeting.

cards.meaning: to encourage or arouse interest or activity
cards.example: The new policy stimulated economic growth.

cards.meaning: to be enough for a purpose
cards.example: A brief explanation will suffice for now.

cards.meaning: to maintain or keep something going over time
cards.example: We need to sustain our efforts to protect the environment.

cards.meaning: to combine ideas or substances into a new whole
cards.example: Good writers can synthesize information from many sources.

cards.meaning: to cause or initiate something to happen
cards.example: Stress can trigger headaches and anxiety.

cards.meaning: to weaken or damage something gradually
cards.example: His constant criticism undermined her confidence.

cards.meaning: to check or confirm that something is true
cards.example: Please verify your email address to complete registration.

cards.meaning: to form a mental image of something
cards.example: Visualize your goal clearly before you begin.
